reports.title: The Clock Trump Can't Stop
reports.subtitle: Iran sent a new peace proposal to Pakistan. Trump called it insufficient. Congress's 60-day War Powers deadline is still ticking.

reports.title: Who Gets to Stay Is Now a Question of Presidential Mood
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court is deciding whether the executive branch can revoke Temporary Protected Status for 700,000 people whenever it wants, with no judicial review.

reports.title: The 5th Circuit Rewrites Who Gets an Abortion
reports.subtitle: A federal appeals court just restored an in-person requirement for mifepristone, cutting off mail-order access for women in rural and restricted-access states.

reports.title: The Special Relationship, Invoiced
reports.subtitle: UK exports to the US fell 25% after Liberation Day tariffs. Britain now runs a trade deficit with its largest trading partner. Polymarket says there is only a 22% chance of a deal before 2027.

reports.title: The Workers Who Could Break the AI Boom
reports.subtitle: Samsung's largest-ever labor action threatens to disrupt HBM memory production at the exact moment AI chip demand has outpaced every forecast.

reports.title: Trump Pulls 5,000 Troops from Germany. NATO's Nervous System Just Got Cut.
reports.subtitle: The withdrawal is not about Germany's defense spending. It is punishment for Merz saying the obvious about Iran.

reports.title: Reform Is Set to Win the Most Council Seats. Britain's Two-Party System Just Broke.
reports.subtitle: Thursday's UK local elections are the first test of whether Reform's poll lead translates into power. Polymarket says 87.9% chance they win the most seats. Labour could lose three-quarters of theirs.

reports.title: The Lebanon Ceasefire Is Holding on Paper and Collapsing on the Ground
reports.subtitle: Thirteen people were killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon on Friday. Hezbollah fired back. Both sides are calling this a ceasefire.

reports.title: Starmer Considers Banning Protests Three Days Before an Election He Is About to Lose
reports.subtitle: Two Jewish men were stabbed in Golders Green this week. The Prime Minister says some pro-Palestinian marches may need to stop. His party is braced to lose up to 2,000 council seats on Thursday.

reports.title: Trump Rips Up the EU Car Deal and Announces 25% Auto Tariffs. Europe Has No Good Response.
reports.subtitle: The US-EU trade agreement was supposed to hold at a 10% baseline tariff. Trump has now raised the rate on cars and lorries to 25%, accusing the EU of non-compliance. The EU has no leverage it is willing to use.
